verdict_reasoning: EREG (Epiregulin) is a member of the EGF family synthesized as a type I transmembrane precursor (pro-epiregulin) that undergoes ectodomain shedding by ADAM proteases (ADAM10/17) to release a soluble ~47 aa mature ligand. The transmembrane pro-form is transiently expressed but rapidly shed; the dominant circulating/acting species is soluble. The pro-form itself is inside-out topology (N-term extracellular, C-term intracellular) and is a short-lived intermediate. Once shed, the soluble ligand binds EGFR/ErbB4 transiently on neighboring cells — this is paracrine, not stable surface residence on the producing cell. It does not meet the wash-resistance test for stable surface accessibility of the protein body on producer cells.
